#
# Phlix Hub one-shot installer for Ubuntu/Debian.
#
# Installs system packages, creates the MySQL database + user, fetches the
# application code, writes the environment file, generates a JWT secret,
# runs database migrations, installs a systemd service, and (optionally)
# sets up an HAProxy reverse proxy with a Let's Encrypt certificate that
# auto-renews monthly.
#
# Usage:
#   sudo bash install.sh [options]
#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/detain/phlix-hub/master/scripts/install.sh | sudo bash
#
# Run with --help for the full option list.

set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Defaults (override via flags or interactive prompts)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
REPO_URL="https://github.com/detain/phlix-hub.git"
BRANCH="master"
INSTALL_PATH="/opt/phlix-hub"
SERVICE_USER="www-data"
ENV_FILE="/etc/phlix-hub.env"
SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/phlix-hub.service"

DB_HOST="127.0.0.1"
DB_PORT="3306"
DB_NAME="phlix_hub"
DB_USER="phlix_hub"
DB_PASS=""              # generated if empty

HUB_PORT="8800"
RELAY_PORT="8802"
CLIENT_RELAY_PORT="8803"
HUB_WORKERS="4"

DOMAIN=""               # public hostname; enables TLS when set with --admin-email
ADMIN_EMAIL=""          # Let's Encrypt registration email
JWT_SECRET=""           # generated if empty

WANT_TLS="auto"         # auto|yes|no
SKIP_HAPROX

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Reverse proxy (HAProxy) + TLS (certbot)

if [ "$SKIP_HAPROXY" = "yes" ]; then
  log "Skipping HAProxy / certbot setup (--no-proxy)"
  info "Run your own reverse proxy in front of:"
  info "  127.0.0.1:${HUB_PORT}  (HTTP API + SSR pages + /health)"
  info "  127.0.0.1:${CLIENT_RELAY_PORT}  (GET /client/{server_id}, WebSocket)"
  info "Servers connect to 127.0.0.1:${RELAY_PORT} for their outbound tunnels."
  info "See README.md → 'Running alongside phlix-server' for a sample shared HAProxy config."

  if command -v ufw >/dev/null 2>&1 && ufw status 2>/dev/null | grep -q "Status: active"; then
    ufw allow "$HUB_PORT"/tcp comment 'Phlix Hub HTTP' >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    ufw allow "$RELAY_PORT"/tcp comment 'Phlix Hub server tunnels' >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    info "Opened ports ${HUB_PORT}/tcp and ${RELAY_PORT}/tcp in ufw."
  fi
else
# Existing reverse-proxy artefacts from a sibling project (e.g. phlix-server
# installed first) — warn and stop, to avoid clobbering the shared config.
if [ -f /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg ] \
   && grep -qE 'be_phlix_server' /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg 2>/dev/null; then

  warn "config by hand — see README.md → 'Running alongside phlix-server'."

fi

tool_input.new_string:
### Running alongside phlix-hub on the same server

Both installers ship their own HAProxy config and would overwrite each other if run with the
default proxy setup. To co-host them, install one normally and the other with `--no-proxy`,
then write a single shared HAProxy config that routes by `Host:` header:

```bash
# 1. Install phlix-hub first (writes /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg with its frontend).
sudo bash install.sh --domain hub.example.com --admin-email you@example.com -y

# 2. Install phlix-server with --no-proxy so it leaves HAProxy alone.
#    It binds 127.0.0.1:8096 and you proxy to it manually.
sudo bash /var/www/phlix/scripts/install.sh --no-proxy -y
```

Each installer also refuses to overwrite `/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg` if it detects the other
service's backend already in place — you'll get a clear error pointing at `--no-proxy`.

Then write `/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg` by hand to route both services:

```haproxy
global
    log /dev/log local0
    maxconn 4096
    tune.ssl.default-dh-param 2048

defaults
    log     global
    mode    http
    option  httplog
    option  forwardfor
    timeout connect 5s
    timeout client  1h
    timeout server  1h
    timeout tunnel  1h

frontend fe_http
    bind :80
    redirect scheme https code 301

frontend fe_https
    bind :443 ssl crt-list /etc/haproxy/certs.list
    http-request set-header X-Forwarded-Proto https

    # Phlix Hub (hub.example.com) — SSR pages, API, and the /client/{id} WebSocket
    acl is_hub hdr(host) -i hub.example.com
    use_backend be_hub_client_relay if is_hub { path_beg /client/ }
    use_backend be_hub              if is_hub

    # Phlix Media Server (phlix.example.com)
    acl is_phlix hdr(host) -i phlix.example.com
    use_backend be_phlix_server if is_phlix

    default_backend be_phlix_server

backend be_hub
    server hub 127.0.0.1:8800
backend be_hub_client_relay
    server clientrelay 127.0.0.1:8803
backend be_phlix_server
    option http-server-close
    server phlix 127.0.0.1:8096
```

And `/etc/haproxy/certs.list`:

```
/etc/haproxy/certs/hub.example.com.pem
/etc/haproxy/certs/phlix.example.com.pem
```

Note that the **hub server-tunnel port** (`:8802`) is a separate listener — servers connect
directly to that port. Open it on your firewall but **don't** put it behind the HAProxy 80/443
frontend.

Everything else is already namespaced: env files (`/etc/phlix-hub.env` vs `/etc/phlix/env`),
systemd units (`phlix-hub.service` vs `phlix-server.service`), install dirs (`/opt/phlix-hub`
vs `/var/www/phlix`), service users (`www-data` vs `phlix`), MySQL DBs (`phlix_hub` vs
`phlix`), backend ports (8800/8802/8803 vs 8096), and certbot artefacts.

### Manual install (from source)

## Running alongside phlix-server

Both installers ship their own HAProxy config and would overwrite each other if run with the
default proxy setup. To co-host them, install one normally and the other with `--no-proxy`,
then write a single shared HAProxy config that routes by `Host:` header:

```bash
# 1. Install phlix-hub first (writes /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg with its frontend).
sudo bash install.sh --domain hub.example.com --admin-email you@example.com -y

# 2. Install phlix-server with --no-proxy so it leaves HAProxy alone.
sudo bash /var/www/phlix/scripts/install.sh --no-proxy -y
```

Each installer also refuses to overwrite `/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg` if it detects the other
service's backend already in place — you'll get a clear error pointing at `--no-proxy`.

Sample combined `/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg` (route by Host header):

```haproxy
global
    log /dev/log local0
    maxconn 4096
    tune.ssl.default-dh-param 2048

defaults
    log     global
    mode    http
    option  httplog
    option  forwardfor
    timeout connect 5s
    timeout client  1h
    timeout server  1h
    timeout tunnel  1h

frontend fe_http
    bind :80
    redirect scheme https code 301

frontend fe_https
    bind :443 ssl crt-list /etc/haproxy/certs.list
    http-request set-header X-Forwarded-Proto https

    # Phlix Hub
    acl is_hub hdr(host) -i hub.example.com
    use_backend be_hub_client_relay if is_hub { path_beg /client/ }
    use_backend be_hub              if is_hub

    # Phlix Media Server
    acl is_phlix hdr(host) -i phlix.example.com
    use_backend be_phlix_server if is_phlix

    default_backend be_hub

backend be_hub
    server hub 127.0.0.1:8800
backend be_hub_client_relay
    server clientrelay 127.0.0.1:8803
backend be_phlix_server
    option http-server-close
    server phlix 127.0.0.1:8096
```

And `/etc/haproxy/certs.list`:

```
/etc/haproxy/certs/hub.example.com.pem
/etc/haproxy/certs/phlix.example.com.pem
```

The **hub server-tunnel port** (`:8802`) is a separate listener — servers connect to that port
directly. Open it on the firewall but **don't** put it behind the HAProxy 80/443 frontend.

Everything else is already namespaced (env files, systemd units, install dirs, service users,
MySQL DBs, backend ports, certbot artefacts) so there are no other co-install conflicts.

## Quick start (development)

## Quick start (development)","newString":"System packages (`php-*`, `mysql-server`, `haproxy`, `certbot`) and `ufw` rules are left in
place — uninstall them yourself with `apt remove` / `ufw delete` if you no longer need them.

## Running alongside phlix-server

Both installers ship their own HAProxy config and would overwrite each other if run with the
default proxy setup. To co-host them, install one normally and the other with `--no-proxy`,
then write a single shared HAProxy config that routes by `Host:` header:

```bash
# 1. Install phlix-hub first (writes /etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg with its frontend).
sudo bash install.sh --domain hub.example.com --admin-email you@example.com -y

# 2. Install phlix-server with --no-proxy so it leaves HAProxy alone.
sudo bash /var/www/phlix/scripts/install.sh --no-proxy -y
```

Each installer also refuses to overwrite `/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg` if it detects the other
service's backend already in place — you'll get a clear error pointing at `--no-proxy`.

Sample combined `/etc/haproxy/haproxy.cfg` (route by Host header):

```haproxy
global
    log /dev/log local0
    maxconn 4096
    tune.ssl.default-dh-param 2048

defaults
    log     global
    mode    http
    option  httplog
    option  forwardfor
    timeout connect 5s
    timeout client  1h
    timeout server  1h
    timeout tunnel  1h

frontend fe_http
    bind :80
    redirect scheme https code 301

frontend fe_https
    bind :443 ssl crt-list /etc/haproxy/certs.list
    http-request set-header X-Forwarded-Proto https

    # Phlix Hub
    acl is_hub hdr(host) -i hub.example.com
    use_backend be_hub_client_relay if is_hub { path_beg /client/ }
    use_backend be_hub

feat(install): dedicated phlix-hub system user + phlix-shared 0.6.0

  schema that's needed at runtime.

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(install): grant on @localhost too, not just @127.0.0.1

PHP/PDO connects to MySQL via TCP at 127.0.0.1, but MySQL reverse-DNSes
that to 'localhost' (per /etc/hosts) before checking user grants. Only
having 'phlix'@'127.0.0.1' makes PDO connections fail with "Access
denied for user 'phlix'@'localhost'" even with the correct password
and host. Adding the matching 'phlix'@'localhost' grant covers both
paths so PDO authenticates regardless of how MySQL resolves the IP.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

tool_response.stdout:
<?php

declare(strict_types=1);

/**
 * Phlix Hub HTTP entry point.
 *
 * Loads composer autoload, primes the static logger + DB pools, builds
 * the PSR-11 container, then hands off to {@see \Phlix\Hub\Application::boot()}
 * which starts a Workerman HTTP worker.
 *
 * Run with `php public/index.php start` during development.
 *
 * @package Phlix\Hub
 */

use Phlix\Hub\Application;
use Phlix\Hub\Common\Container\ContainerFactory;
use Phlix\Hub\Common\Container\Providers\HubServicesProvider;
use Phlix\Hub\Common\Database\ConnectionPool;
use Phlix\Hub\Common\Logger\LoggerFactory;

require_once __DIR__ . '/../vendor/autoload.php';

$configDir = __DIR__ . '/../config';
$dbConfigPath = $configDir . '/database.php';
$loggerConfigPath = $configDir . '/logger.php';
$authConfigPath = $configDir . '/auth.php';

LoggerFactory::init($loggerConfigPath);
// We deliberately do NOT call ConnectionPool::init() at bootstrap so the
// /health endpoint stays reachable even when MySQL is unreachable. The
// pool is initialised lazily by the container the first time a service
// asks for a Connection.
ConnectionPool::class; // ensure autoload pulls the class.

/** @var array<string, mixed> $serverConfig */
$serverConfig = include $configDir . '/server.php';
$serverConfig['db_config_path'] = $dbConfigPath;
$serverConfig['logger_config_path'] = $loggerConfigPath;
$serverConfig['auth_config_path'] = $authConfigPath;

$container = ContainerFactory::create($serverConfig);

// Register container for runtime timer wiring in HubServicesProvider::boot()
HubServicesProvider::setContainer($container);

$app = new Application($container, $serverConfig);
$app->boot();

feat(workerman): root-level start.php (webman-style) + /assets/ static serving

Mirrors phlix-server's new layout so both projects boot the same way:

  start.php (NEW, repo root)
    Thin bootstrap — composer autoload, LoggerFactory::init(), build
    the PSR-11 container from config/server.php + injected db / logger
    / auth config paths, then hand off to Application::boot() which
    creates the HTTP worker on port 8800, the server-tunnel relay on
    8802, the client-tunnel relay on 8803, and calls Worker::runAll().

  public/index.php
    Reduced to a one-line shim that requires start.php. Anything still
    pointing at `php public/index.php start` (older systemd units,
    docs, muscle memory) keeps working unchanged. Marked @deprecated
    so future readers route to start.php directly.

  src/Application.php — Application::boot()
    Added a static-file fast path in the onMessage closure so requests
    under public/ (CSS, JS, images, fonts referenced from base.tpl
    like `/assets/css/app.css`) are served directly without going
    through the router. Refuses traversal via realpath() prefix check
    and never serves raw .php files. The document root is read from
    config['public_root'] (set by start.php to __DIR__ . '/public');
    falls back to <repo>/public so tests that don't pass it through
    still work.

  scripts/install.sh
    Systemd ExecStart now invokes start.php directly:
      ExecStart=/usr/bin/php ${INSTALL_PATH}/start.php start

Ports unchanged — phlix-hub still binds 8800/8802/8803, phlix-server
binds 8096/8097/1900; the two projects' worker ports stay

#
# Phlix Hub one-shot installer for Ubuntu/Debian.
#
# Installs system packages, creates the MySQL database + user, fetches the
# application code, writes the environment file, generates a JWT secret,
# runs database migrations, installs a systemd service, and (optionally)
# sets up an HAProxy reverse proxy with a Let's Encrypt certificate that
# auto-renews monthly.
#
# Usage:
#   sudo bash install.sh [options]
#   curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/detain/phlix-hub/master/scripts/install.sh | sudo bash
#
# Run with --help for the full option list.

set -euo pipefail

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Defaults (override via flags or interactive prompts)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
REPO_URL="https://github.com/detain/phlix-hub.git"
BRANCH="master"
INSTALL_PATH="/opt/phlix-hub"
# Default to a dedicated system user. Existing installs that were created
# with `www-data` keep running as-is — `--update` reads User= from the
# systemd unit rather than touching it.
SERVICE_USER="phlix-hub"
ENV_FILE="/etc/phlix-hub.env"
SERVICE_FILE="/etc/systemd/system/phlix-hub.service"

DB_HOST="127.0.0.1"
DB_PORT="3306"
DB_N

# 4b. One-off migration: if this install pre-dates the fragment-based
  # HAProxy layout, convert it now. Idempotent on subsequent updates.
  phlix_haproxy_migrate_if_needed_hub

  # 5. Refresh the systemd unit in case ExecStart / WorkingDirectory drifted,
  # then restart. We don't touch the env file.
  if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
    log "Restarting phlix-hub service"
    systemctl daemon-reload >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl restart phlix-hub
    sleep 2
    if systemctl is-active --quiet phlix-hub; then
      info "phlix-hub service is running."
    else
      warn "phlix-hub did not start cleanly — check 'journalctl -u phlix-hub -e'."
    fi
  else
    warn "No systemd unit at $SERVICE_FILE — start the hub manually."
  fi

  # 6. Health check.
  local hub_port="${env_hub_port:-8800}"
  if curl -fsS --max-time 5 "http://localhost:${hub_port}/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    info "Health check OK: http://localhost:${hub_port}/health"
  else

tool_input.new_string:
# 5. Migrate the systemd unit if it's pre-`start.php`. Older installs
  # had `ExecStart=… public/index.php start`; the new entry point is
  # start.php at the repo root. We only touch the ExecStart line so any
  # operator customisation elsewhere in the unit is preserved.
  if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ] && grep -q 'public/index\.php start' "$SERVICE_FILE" 2>/dev/null; then
    log "Migrating systemd unit ExecStart to start.php"
    sed -i 's|public/index\.php start|start.php start|' "$SERVICE_FILE"
    systemctl daemon-reload >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  fi

  # 6. Restart the service. We don't touch the env file.
  if [ -f "$SERVICE_FILE" ]; then
    log "Restarting phlix-hub service"
    systemctl daemon-reload >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    systemctl restart phlix-hub
    sleep 2
    if systemctl is-active --quiet phlix-hub; then
      info "phlix-hub service is running."
    else
      warn "phlix-hub did not start cleanly — check 'journalctl -u phlix-hub -e'."
    fi
  else
    warn "No systemd unit at $SERVICE_FILE — start the hub manually."
  fi

file.content:
# 6. systemd service
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
log "Installing systemd service"
cat > "$SERVICE_FILE" <<EOF
[Unit]
Description=Phlix Hub
After=network.target mysql.service

[Service]
Type=simple
User=${SERVICE_USER}
Group=${SERVICE_USER}
EnvironmentFile=${ENV_FILE}
WorkingDirectory=${INSTALL_PATH}
# start.php is the Workerman bootstrap (webman-style — see start.php
# for the full pattern). public/index.php no longer exists; public/ is
# a pure document root containing only static assets + templates.
ExecStart=/usr/bin/php ${INSTALL_PATH}/start.php start
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=5

tool_input.command:
git add public/index.php start.php scripts/install.sh
git status --short
echo "==="
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
refactor(workerman): drop public/index.php shim; start.php is the only entry

public/index.php is gone. It existed as a one-line `require __DIR__ .
'/../start.php'` shim so existing systemd units pointing at the old
`public/index.php start` invocation would keep working. That backward
compatibility isn't worth carrying — anyone running this on an
existing install will run --update, and:

  - scripts/install.sh do_update() now rewrites the systemd unit's
    ExecStart line via `sed` when it detects the legacy
    `public/index.php start` form, switching it to `start.php start`
    and triggering a daemon-reload. Other operator customisations in
    the unit are preserved.

  - The install.sh inline systemd template's ExecStart already points
    at start.php, so fresh installs use the canonical entry directly.

public/ is now a pure document root — assets/ and templates/ only,
which is what the static-file fast path in Application::boot() expects.

Also pin `Worker::$logFile` to the install's `.logs/` directory so the
process never tries to write `workerman.log` into the cwd. phlix-hub
doesn't yet harden its systemd unit with `ProtectSystem=strict`, but
phlix-server already does and ran into the same default-log-path EROFS
the moment its strict unit shipped — pin it explicitly so this repo
doesn't hit the same trap when hardening lands here.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -3
unset GITHUB_TOKEN
git push origin master 2>&1 | tail -3

fix(application): satisfy psalm + phpcs on static-file fast path

CI surfaced four issues in the onMessage closure added by the previous
commit:

- src/Application.php:577 MixedAssignment — explicit `@var mixed` on
  the $publicRootRaw read so psalm sees the source as mixed before
  the is_string/is_dir narrowing.
- src/Application.php:601 RedundantCastGivenDocblockType — pathinfo()
  with PATHINFO_EXTENSION already returns string; the (string) cast
  was extra.
- src/Application.php:604 RiskyTruthyFalsyComparison — the elvis
  operator on `false|string` (mime_content_type return) triggered
  psalm's truthy-vs-string warning. Replace with an explicit
  is_string && !== '' guard so the type narrowing is plain.
- PSR12 multi-line if formatting — first expression must be on its
  own line after the opening paren. Move `$real !== false` down.

No behavioural change; all 558 tests still pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
